description_long: This sculpture depicts a cherubic child seated on an ornate pedestal, with one leg crossed over the other. The child is draped in a classical robe, leaving the upper torso and one leg exposed, and holds a round object in the right hand, possibly a mirror or medallion. The left hand rests on the pedestal, which features decorative elements and a stepped base. The figure's hair is styled in soft curls, and the facial expression is serene and contemplative. The back of the sculpture reveals detailed folds in the drapery and the continuation of the pedestal's ornamentation. The entire piece appears to be crafted from a single block of stone, with a weathered, matte finish.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of a cherubic child seated on an ornate pedestal, draped in classical robes and holding a round object.
description_view_0: Front view showing the child's face, crossed legs, draped robe, and the round object held in the right hand.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the child's face, the drapery, and the side of the pedestal.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the child's head, detailed folds of the robe, and the ornate back of the pedestal.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the child's arm resting on the pedestal and the side details of the base.
attribute_subject_matter: seated cherubic child holding a round object
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture could be placed in a park, plaza, or garden.
